title: Can you break your salah for a medical emergency in the masjid?
tags: can you break prayer for emergency
someone faints in masjid during salah
is it allowed to break salah
stanceSummary: It is not only permissible but obligatory to break one's salah to attend to a medical emergency. If someone faints or has a medical emergency during congregational prayer, the one or two people nearest should immediately break their salah to help. If a doctor is present in another row, others have every right to walk through the rows and tap that person to break their prayer. Saving a life takes priority over completing salah, which can always be prayed later. Not everyone should break their prayer — only those needed to handle the situation.
alternateQuestions: Is it permissible to break prayer if someone collapses in the masjid?
Can I walk through prayer rows during an emergency?
Should I break my salah to help someone having a medical emergency?
Is saving a life more important than salah?
What should you do if someone faints during congregational prayer?
transcript:
## Summary of Yasir Qadhi's Position

In a [2025 video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mrY7Yqw7eSE) (starting at [35:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mrY7Yqw7eSE&t=2100s)), Yasir Qadhi addressed what to do when a medical emergency occurs during congregational prayer. His position:

- **It is obligatory** — not merely allowed — to break your salah for a medical emergency.
- The one or two people **nearest the person** should immediately break their salah and assess the situation.
- If a **doctor is present** in another row, others have every right to walk through the rows and tap the doctor to break their salah.
- **Not everyone** should break their prayer — only those needed to handle the emergency.
- Your salah is **not more important than someone's life** — you can always pray later.

---

## Full Lecture Transcript (Cleaned)

### The Question — [35:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mrY7Yqw7eSE&t=2100s)

If somebody faints during the salah but the physician is in another row, are we allowed to walk through and cut the other person's salah? Like, basically it happened in another masjid apparently — I don't know, in Dallas or somewhere else — can you explain what we should do in case an emergency happens?

### The Ruling — [35:15](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mrY7Yqw7eSE&t=2115s)

This is a very good and necessary question. If somebody faints in the salah, the **one or two people next to that person should immediately break their salah** and see: is it something like a fainting spell where they recover and just need help getting up, or is it an actual legitimate emergency? If it is an actual emergency, they will call 911.

If they see a doctor — because our community members know each other — if they see a doctor in the masjid, **they have every right to walk through the rows and tap the person on the shoulder** and say, "Please break your salah. There's an emergency back here." And that person should break the salah and come and examine.

**It is completely not just allowed — it is obligatory.** Brothers, your salah is not more important than a life. You can pray later. You can pray later.

### Practical Guidelines — [36:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mrY7Yqw7eSE&t=2160s)

When a medical emergency happens — the people, not everybody — again, another thing: too many people break their salah. No. If you see a person is taking care of the one who fainted, the job is done, unless you're a doctor. The job is done. And if you cannot find another doctor there, call 911, take the person outside if you can.

If you need more people, say to somebody there, "I need your help. Can you break your salah?" **You can speak to the person.** Get the help you need — two people carry the person outside — whatever the medical emergency needs to be done.

But without a doubt, **this is something the Shariah wants you to do**. Understand: not only is there no sin — it is obligatory to do so.
